title: Der Mond
wikitext:
[]: Der Mond             Quote Post by Mr.Guy » Sat Aug 28, 2004 5:45 pm Not entirely sure if this should go here... but whatever... Does anyone have this collection of art work? It's a book put together by Yoshiyuki Sadamoto, and as such, has plenty of his work in it (70 pages of Eva art). Now my question... does anybody have this and/or can recommend it? It isn't that expensive, but a recommendation is always nice. Or does anyone know where I could get scans (I've checked google)? Any answers would be greatly appreciated

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Sat Aug 28, 2004 6:45 pm I'm probably the only other person here who doesn't have this book. What I can tell you, however, is that Die Sterne Is most definitely not worth it... of course, that book only cost me $48.00, but it's just a collection of graphics that are easily found online. I think that EvaMonkey.com's old format had frequent random appearances of Mond pics in it's upper left-hand corner, but I'm not sure. However, I'm sure that whatever's in Mond is probably stored somewhere online. Any comments from someone in the know?

[]:             Quote Post by Nephilim » Sun Aug 29, 2004 4:28 pm i like to have all those images in my computer :P i have to adapt to my lack of money hehe The only sadamoto art that i don't have is from the cover and box of the platinum edition.. Does someone have this images???, they look very nice to me

[]:             Quote Post by Mr.Guy » Sun Aug 29, 2004 8:41 pm Thanks for the advice/info Hexon, much appreciated :P Nephilim, do you know where I could also find those shots? I'd rather not have to spend the money as well... could you direct me to a site?

[]:             Quote Post by ShoKenju » Sun Aug 29, 2004 8:44 pm or better yet, host them all and give them to us ^_________^

[]:             Quote Post by Nephilim » Sun Aug 29, 2004 10:52 pm Currently i'm working in the best spanish evangelion site, with the bigger image gallery in the world. I hope i finish the website soon :P For now, here i have all the sadamoto pictures that i have, zipped in a 10 mb file. [url]http://www.med.ufro.cl/tecmed/cachureos/claudio/Sadamoto.zip[/url] i hope you like it

[]:             Quote Post by ShoKenju » Sun Aug 29, 2004 11:04 pm Nephilim wrote: Currently i'm working in the best spanish evangelion site, with the bigger image gallery in the world. I hope i finish the website soon :P For now, here i have all the sadamoto pictures that i have, zipped in a 10 mb file. [url]http://www.med.ufro.cl/tecmed/cachureos/claudio/Sadamoto.zip[/url] i hope you like it

[]:             Quote Post by Nephilim » Mon Aug 30, 2004 12:39 am what? you can't download 10 mb? is not too much really :roll: even if you are conected by the phone line

[]:             Quote Post by ShoKenju » Mon Aug 30, 2004 12:43 am lets put it this way, i usually start downloading at a full 2m/s then it drops to a stable 200k/s this just went from 30 to 20. and i use wireless, so downloads periodically stop. causing me to delete and retry til its finished

[]:             Quote Post by Mr.Guy » Mon Aug 30, 2004 8:18 am O_O Thank you very much Neph... I don't have most of this art. Would make some nice avatars :) . The download only took me 20 seconds, you should retry it ShoKenju. I think I still might pick up the book... just to have them in tangible form. Does anyone here actually own the book?

[]:             Quote Post by Nephilim » Mon Aug 30, 2004 12:09 pm It took you 20 secs??? :shock: Damm !!!!, it took me half an hour to upload it !!! :roll: Well, some have more luck thatn others... About the pictures, i have no idea wich ones are from that book, so if somebody have some sadamoto images that i don't have, it would be nice to see them :P

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Mon Aug 30, 2004 6:45 pm Which pics? Here's a tip aboout the contents of Die Sterne . If you've ever seen an official Evangleion picture somewhere online, whether it's a poster, a T-shirt, or one of those odder wallscrolls, it's in Die Sterne . The only real exceptions would be stuff that's been made since it was published, which I, for one, have never seen. Oh, and there are no game screenshots, it's simply graphics. Those UCC adds? In there. Even that chilling depiction of Rei in the bikini's in there. Also within is a thin section devoted to some of the better works of fan art. From what's I'm hearing about Der Mond , it also contains some art from Nadia . But then, I'm sure you already knew that.

[]:             Quote Post by Asuka Langley » Mon Aug 30, 2004 9:00 pm Yep, it's got a lot of his Eva works in it. They're all well known images now. Also, as Hexon said, there's a few Nadia images, and a few miscellaneous other images as well. I have the entire thing scanned... :insert hint ninja here:

[]:             Quote Post by Mr.Guy » Tue Aug 31, 2004 6:19 pm Thanks for the info everyone... I think I'll hold off on the book for now, maybe pick it up later if I ever get the extra cash. @Asuka: PM thingy sent.

[]:             Quote Post by Bean Bandit » Mon May 21, 2007 6:13 pm i bought it a last year. A bit pricey (i think it was $28) but a great collection of images. Coming from someone who creates his own manga regularly, its fun to look at it from a stylistic standpoint. Its cool to compare his COPiC stuff to his Painter/Photoshop stuff. I'm pretty sure the first Painter pic is that one of early one of Nadia in yet another revealing outfit...the early sketches of the eva characters are fun, too. Good book, overall.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon May 21, 2007 9:17 pm Please don't double or triple post. You can always click the checkbox for "Disable Smilies in this post" below the text box.

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Fri Sep 07, 2007 6:19 pm I have this book! It's really quite fantastic, actually. I had seen most of the pictures before, in some shape or form, but having them all gathered together in stunning high-quality, complete with notes from Sadamoto is great! I often just take it out and spend a while looking through it! I got it for about â‚¬13, which is NOTHING, considering what the release is (a massive, hardcover book). I say: Get it!
